content.divergence_summary: Same event, opposite outcomes: main was at the east channel and the stag's path brought it there — object obtained, attention sharpened. Alt2 was too far east pursuing the colony; the signal arrived late, the run cost health, and the stag was gone by arrival. The east push that defines alt2's arc is the direct cause of the miss.
content.branch_a_path: Positioned at east channel; witnessed the stag; acquired pale fur tuft. Food slightly down, attention up. First inventory item.
content.branch_b_path: At the east clearing when the signal arrived; ran west fast; arrived too late. Impressions in the ground only. Health set back by exertion during recovery. No object.
key_differences: Main obtained a physical trace from the visitor; alt2 obtained only the trace of the trace — impressions in ground, unsettled birds
Alt2's health dropped (0.57 → 0.54) from the run; main's held near-steady (0.78 → 0.77)
Alt2's attention fell sharply (0.83 → 0.72) after a failed pursuit; main's rose (0.74 → 0.82) after a successful one
The colony goal continues to pull alt2 east, which is the structural reason it missed today's event
shared_elements: Both heard the same bird-call sequence
Both moved toward the east path in response
Both ended with the stag having passed and continued — neither branch had any interaction with the visitor directly
